</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">column</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {...}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Using ColumnByExpressionView to model the custom column Weight:</span><br /><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Weight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnByExpressionView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; column {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTableCell</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RealCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTableCell</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LiteralInteger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ? (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LiteralInteger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">value</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">else</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                            (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LiteralRational</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">value</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getWeight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">e</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    (((</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">e</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ownedElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AttributeUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">)-&gt;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ControlFunctions::select</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ee</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                        (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ee</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">name</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;weight&quot;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    } </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AttributeUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ownedRelationship</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FeatureValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">value</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Price</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnByExpressionView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; column {...}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Net Price'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColumnByExpressionView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; column {...}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Variant Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VariantTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartDefinition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Definition::isVariation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneVariants</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modeling_ColumnByExpressionView.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Other Model-Based Tabular Views &amp; Columns Improvements</h4><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li data-uuid="329559b4-0197-4e07-b9ea-a2d0b878499f">A new toolbar button has been added to tabular views, allowing you to open the current table view directly in the Textual Editor.</li><li data-uuid="192ea281-7975-4712-bbc3-b8990c7a0b79">The <em>isMultiline</em> attribute has been introduced for single-valued string cells, enabling text wrapping and multiline display. <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="556ecce7-2ea1-415d-a661-a5fa45b94c84">Editing of primitive <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em> cell values (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60858666-bb50-4245-9efe-c9b897380295">e.g., String, Element, Enum, Integer, Real, Boolean</ac:inline-comment-marker>) is now supported directly within the graphical representation of the table view.</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Other Model-Based Tabular Views &amp; Columns Improvements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <strong>Open in Textual Editor </strong>button; editing of the String value in a cell of the <em>Declared Name</em> column; text wrapping in the <em>Qualified Name </em>column cells via the <em>isMultiline </em>attribute.</h6><h3>View Creation Dialog Customization</h3><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Model-Based View Creation Dialog</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can now model a custom, model-based View Creation dialog that lists only the view creation commands</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> of your choice.</span></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">There are two options for customizing the View Creation dialog:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="9a827616-2ee3-4119-8eea-78035a5be441"><ul><li data-uuid="a9d61dda-14de-44ab-ba8c-c6368461717e"><p>Customize the predefined <em style="text-align: left;">SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>configuration in the <em>SysML Customization</em> model library. This allows you to inherit its configuration and tailor the View Creation dialog as needed. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="c039f97c-ce22-4e86-a073-6241b54caf06"><p>Create a View Creation dialog from scratch <span>via the conceptual <em>ViewCreationDialog </em>definition</span>. This gives you full control of designing the dialog entirely according to your own needs — without relying on predefined settings. <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">With either approach, you can define a model-based View Creation dialog that includes only the commands you need, as well as specifying how they are structured into custom command categories. This enables full customization of the dialog to match the needs of your specific project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model-Based View Creation Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image on the left shows the View Creation submenu according to the predefined <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> configuration. The image on the right shows the customized View Creation submenu.  </h6><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">View Creation Dialog Management</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Additionally, you can activate different View Creation dialog configurations for your project via the <strong>Active Create View Dialog</strong>. Use the <strong>Manage Create View Dialog</strong> command from the configuration element shortcut menu or the <strong>Active View Creation Dialog</strong> project option. While the predefined <em style="text-align: left;">SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> configuration is enabled by default for all SysML v2 projects, you can easily activate another configuration. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="View Creation Dialog Management.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;">The <strong>Manage Create View Dialog</strong> command invokes the <strong>Active Create View Dialog</strong>, where the custom View Creation dialog's <em>CustomViewCreationDialog </em>configuration is activated for the project.</h6><h3 style="text-align: left;">Cross Features Support</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Cross features are now supported in the modeling tool, allowing you to </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">specify that a value at one end of the connection is linked to a value at the other end. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can specify cross features for connection ends as owned cross features or declare them in the related types of the connection using the cross subsetting relationship. Owned cross features can be named or unnamed. Cross features in connection usages support both full and shorthand notations.</span> </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c4c3354-4a75-4b25-899d-b9b6745028ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* cross features owned by the connection definition ends */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">..*] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NetworkConfiguration</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkConnections</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    } </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cross_feature_support.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li data-uuid="2598a84c-b7ba-41ce-af4e-52ccedb36012">Additionally, <strong>Move Cross Feature to End </strong>and <strong>Move Cross Feature to End Type </strong>refactoring commands are available for connection definition ends, allowing you to convert ends between the two declaration options.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cross_features_refactoring.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 style="text-align: left;">SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Progress</h3><ul><li data-uuid="98c98c20-66d0-456d-ae10-ed24c75b1d34">The <strong>SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration</strong> plugin has been enhanced, now supporting a broader range of elements. However, as the plugin is still a work in progress, not all SysML v1 elements are currently supported for migration. <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Performing SysML v1 to v2 model transformation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Progress.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A SysML v1 model and its result after migration to SysML v2.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="7b5002e5-3de8-4891-8b47-beed6f5c6382"><span>Additionally, if the migration includes any failures, a .<em>xlsx</em> migration status file is now generated. This file lists separate sheets for elements and properties that were not migrated. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Performing SysML v1 to v2 model transformation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration.png" /></ac:image></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Style Sheet Improvements</h3><h4>Style Sheet Management Improvements</h4><ul><li data-uuid="0e45ebf8-ad33-47f7-88c2-39e822040fd3">You can now activate style sheets for both the entire project and for individual views. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This flexibility allows you to combine multiple style sheets based on your modeling needs (for example, activating a style sheet for the entire project while activating distinct style sheets for individual views). Additionally, style sheets activated at both the project and view levels can be prioritized by setting their order of application. You can activate style sheets either via the Textual Editor or using the newly introduced <strong>Style Sheets</strong> button in the view toolbar. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Style Sheet Improvements 1.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>is activated for the entire project via the <strong>Style Sheets </strong>button in the view toolbar, while the <em>NewStyle</em><em> </em>style sheet, which changes the color of an interface usage, is activated for the specific view <em>vehicle</em>. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="38e55d56-b392-4c1e-ac26-bfaeb390b7d5"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can also disable project-level style sheets for a specific view.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Style Sheet Improvements 2.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Disabling active project style sheets for the <em>vehicle </em>view via the <strong>Apply Style Sheet </strong>dialog.</span></h6><h4>Other Style Sheet Improvements</h4><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A new rule condition, <em>FreeFormCondition, </em>has been introduced for style sheet rules, allowing you to target element symbols by the specified expression. <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li style="text-align: left;">Style sheet part definitions can now be displayed as symbols in views, featuring a dedicated compartment that lists their rules—each accompanied by an icon that visually reflects the defined style.<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="defaultstylesheetpartdefinition.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Semantic and Non-Semantic Metadata Representation</h3><p>As two kinds of metadata can be applied to model elements—semantic and non-semantic—representational differences have been implemented in accordance with the SysML v2 language specification. The following differences apply to element representation <span>in the Containment tree, Specification panel, symbols on views, and any other UI instances. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metadata" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p>If an element has at least one semantic metadata applied to it:</p><ul><li data-uuid="df0a688c-b0e0-4f5f-8cfd-01f38031f884">The element's metaclass keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is not displayed.</li><li data-uuid="ddc87aa6-65d0-4b68-ba3b-5f3fea0ba123">The applied semantic metadata name is displayed with the # sign within guillemets « ». The <em>def</em> keyword is appended for definition elements. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="semantic_metadata.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p>If an element has only non-semantic metadata applied to it:</p><ul><li data-uuid="7d37d4c1-8a75-476d-8331-f69f1276a880">The applied semantic metadata name is not displayed; thus, element representation remains essentially unchanged. The element only displays metadata-related information in the metadata compartment and a metadata annotation. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="non-semantic_metadata.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Package Member Display</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This release introduces display options for package members, providing you with greater control and visibility. </span></p><ul><li data-uuid="7c082775-8f44-470f-96b6-aac62aab1701"><p>The new Display Owned Members and Display Owned Members Recursively commands allow you to view package members in both Nested-view and Tree-view styles.</p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display Owned Members commands.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="f31b2650-91c3-4059-a5e3-83ae0f14b530"><p>The package element symbol now supports displaying its members directly within the members compartment, improving the visibility of the package structure.</p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Members compartment.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="f6727132-78d8-482f-bcb2-8141e6d76ab4"><p>A new symbol property, Show Only Owned, has been added for the members compartment. It allows you to choose whether to display only the elements owned by the package or include the imported ones as well.</p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show only owned symbol property.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><h3>Graphical Notation Improvements for Loop and If-Else Action Usages</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This release introduces several graphical notation enhancements for <em>for loops</em>, <em>while loops</em>, and <em>if-else actions</em>, improving clarity and visual consistency:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="2ad9ce6d-b4e8-425e-864e-9c28e5b76bf3">Action conditions — such as <em>if</em>, <em>while</em>, <em>until</em>, and <em>loop </em>iterators — are now shown in dedicated compartments, rather than within the name header.</li><li data-uuid="5b6510fd-6c8c-4808-b6c5-8b0ed2126587">Nested actions are now displayed in graphical compartments, instead of being listed under body, <em>thenClause</em>, or <em>elseClause</em>.</li><li data-uuid="704b4356-2ec8-4437-afad-6bb6b69a6805">As a result, Tree-view style display commands now display nested elements in a Nested-view style within their respective compartments.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Graphical Notation Improvements for Loop and If-Else Action Usages.png" /></ac:image>The <em>if</em> action with the <em>if condition </em>compartment as well as nested actions in <em>thenClause</em> and <em>elseClause </em>compartments.</h6><h3>Navigation for Elements Connected via Interfaces</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5dd01ef3-3bc0-44c7-abb7-9a1adfdc035a">The Go To command</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> now supports navigation between elements connected through their owned or inherited ports via interfac</span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="166d9ff3-0d9d-4fc5-919b-5616531fd446">es</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element navigation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Navigation for Elements Connected via Interfaces.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Specification Panel and Window Improvements</h3><h4>Context-Aware Inherited Features</h4><p>To prevent unintended global model changes, clicking the Navigate button next to an inherited feature now opens that feature in the Specification panel in read-only mode. If you need to modify the default value in the current context, you can click the Redefine button to redefine the feature. <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Context-Aware Inherited Features.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Toggle Library Elements' Visibility in the Specification Window</h4><p>You can now choose to display library elements for properties in the Specification window, providing a more comprehensive view of model elements. <ac:link ac:anchor="Toggling library elements' visibility in the Specification Window"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Toggle Library Elements Visibility in the Specification Window.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Other Specification Panel and Window Improvements</h4><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="f0542a8d-5c9d-4348-9325-8f3cf108f304"><ul><li>Holding down the CTRL key while dragging and dropping elements onto element-typed fields in the Specification panel (e.g., Type, Use Case Included, etc.) now allows you to add the dropped element in addition to the set element.</li><li>The issue where it was possible to change Declared Name, Short Name, Documentation for elements that cannot have those properties (e.g., specializations) has been fixed.</li></ul></li></ul><h3><span>Other Improvements</span></h3><ul><li><span>The View Properties dialog has been introduced, which can be invoked via the View Properties command in the view's shortcut menu. It contains the Use Gradient Fill style property allowing you to specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color symbol style properties. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="55c48910-40e2-4952-9f61-2423ce315e46"><span>New symbol properties have been introduced:</span><ul><li><span>The Show Name symbol property, allowing you to choose whether the element name should be displayed on the element symbol. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="751592c7-d996-4674-8e95-baa9129a734c"><span>The Show Short Name symbol property, allowing you to choose whether the element short name should be displayed on the element symbol. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="f2e99d35-009d-472e-ad7f-5787135b7ac5">The Show Only Owned <span>symbol property, allowing you to specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="c1d16afc-d055-4de3-84c9-5a04315f1e1f">The Show Modifiers <span>symbol property, allowing you to specify whether the element's modifiers (e.g., <em>in, ref, abstract</em>, etc.) should be displayed on the element symbol. The property does not control the individual and portion kind (timeslice, snapshot) modifiers. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="72563b93-9b69-4f5e-b1bb-763be0e0755f">The Text Vertical Position symbol property, allowing you to specify the element name position for the symbol. <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="7d7103ec-d2b6-400e-b65a-e5ec02953fe4">SysML v2 Alpha 1.8</li><li data-uuid="6253c926-2ba3-43c0-b1e9-f5d5dfca484e">SysML v2 Evaluation Alpha 1.8</li><li data-uuid="83416f29-8151-4282-93e9-797c4a7228a4">SysML v2 Collaborative Modeling 1.8</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577879 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00484: Analysis & Verification

- page_id: `249577879`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577879/Analysis+Verification
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:48:19.594+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides basic procedures for working with cases, analysis cases, verification cases, and calculations.

Cases, analysis cases, verification cases, and calculations are generally used within the context of model evaluation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2EP'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This section provides basic procedures for working with cases, analysis cases, verification cases, and calculations.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a24b93b8-15da-4ec1-bed0-1e0ccdd4ae48"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Cases, analysis cases, verification cases, and calculations are generally used within the context of model evaluation. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2EP" ri:content-title="SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577884 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00485: Analysis case

- page_id: `249577884`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577884/Analysis+case
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:55:21.835+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Analysis & Verification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An analysis case is a kind of case whose objective is to carry out an analysis on the subject of the case and return a result about it. The analysis result can be evaluated to determine whether it satisfies the analysis objective. An analysis case is comprised of a set of steps with an objective to evaluate a result about a subject of the analysis.

##### Creating analysis case usage and definition elements

###### Creating analysis case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create analysis case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *analysis def*for an analysis definition.
  2. *analysis* for an analysis usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subjects, objectives, and return parameters for analysis cases' space=''] in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. analysisdefFuelEconomyAnalysis{ // analysis definition 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
returnfuelEconomyResult:DistancePerVolumeValue; 
objectivefuelEconomyAnalysisObjective{ 
doc 
/*The objective of this analysis is to determine whether thesubject vehicle can satisfy the fuel economy requirement.*/ 
requirement:FuelEconomyRequirement; 
} 
// ... 
}analysisfuelEconomyAnalysis:FuelEconomyAnalysis;// analysis usage

###### Creating analysis case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create analysis case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. analysis def to create an analysis definition element.
  2. analysis to create an analysis usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subjects, objectives, and return parameters for analysis cases' space=''] .

###### Auto-creating analysis case definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create analysis case definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577287 space=SYSML2P version=10 -->
## PAGE 00486: Annotations

- page_id: `249577287`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577287/Annotations
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2026-04-20T12:49:09.829+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An annotating element is used to provide additional information about annotated elements. There are several kinds of annotating elements:

- comment is used to provide textual descriptions about other elements. Comments may be about the namespace that owns them, or they may be about different elements, in which case they must be specified.
- documentation is a kind of comment used to document the annotated element. Documentation comments always annotate a single element, which is their owning element.
- textual representation is an annotating element whose textual body provides a representation of the annotated element in a specifically named language (such as SysML textual notation). As for documentation, a textual representation is always owned by its represented element.

The annotated elements can own annotating elements, related via the owning membership relationship. The **comment** and **textual representation**elements can also be related to the annotated elements via the annotation relationship. In symbolic views, you can create or display the annotation and owning membership relationships using the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='']** command between the annotating and annotated elements, which can be represented either as element symbols or compartment elements. The **documentation** element is always related via the owning membership, however, if it is documenting an element other than its owner, the relationship is represented in a view as an annotation.

For a line note in the Textual Editor, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Textual Editor' space=''] page.

##### Creating comments

###### Creating comments via the Textual Editor

To create comments via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed and/or unspecified comment, use symbols /* and */ with the comment text in between.
  2. For a named and/or specified comment, declare the keyword comment , specify its name (optional), then the keyword about followed by the name of the element it annotates (optional), specify the keyword locale followed by the language of the body text and the region and/or encoding, then symbols /* and */ with the comment text in between.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

/* May require revision */ // unnamed commentcommentaboutvehicle/* May require revision */ // unnamed comment about the element 'vehicle' 
commentrevisionsaboutvehicle/* May require revision */ // named comment 'revisions' about the element 'vehicle' commentrevisionsaboutvehiclelocale"en_US"/* May require revision */ // named comment 'revisions' about the element 'vehicle' with specified locale (in English, region US)

###### Creating comments via the Create Element command

To create comments via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click**Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click comment .
3. Specify the comment text.
4. (Optional) .

###### Creating comments via the view palette

To create comments via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click the comment button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the comment symbol.
3. Specify the comment text.
4. (Optional) .

##### Specifying the commented element

###### Specifying the commented element via the Textual Editor

If the comment is an owned member of a namespace, then the explicit identification of annotated elements can be omitted, in which case the annotated element is implicitly the containing namespace.

To specify the commented element explicitly, use the *about* syntax. See the procedure .

commentaboutvehicle/* May require revision */ // unnamed comment about the element 'vehicle' 
commentrevisionsaboutvehicle/* May require revision */ // named comment 'revisions' about the element 'vehicle'

###### Specifying the commented element via the view palette

To specify the commented element via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the annotation button.
2. In the view pane, click on the comment element, then the element you want to annotate with the comment. The annotation relationship is created and the element is annotated with the comment.

###### Specifying the commented element via the Create Relation command

To specify the commented element via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the comment element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click annotation . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to annotate. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to annotate and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to annotate from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

##### Creating documentation

###### Creating documentation via the Textual Editor

To create documentation via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword doc , then specify the name (optional), followed by symbols /* and */ with the documentation text in between.
2. Click the Synchronize button. parttank{ 
doc/* tank is full */ 
}

###### Creating documentation via the Create Element command

To create documentation via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element for which you want to create documentation, and in the shortcut menu, click**Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click documentation .
3. Specify the documentation text.

###### Creating documentation via the view palette

To create documentation via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click the documentation button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the documentation symbol.
3. Specify the documentation text.

###### Creating documentation via the Specification panel

To create documentation via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.

##### Creating textual representation

###### Creating textual representation via the Textual Editor

To create textual representation via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword rep , then specify the name (optional), then the keyword language with the language specified as a literal string, followed by symbols /* and */ with the documentation text in between. If the textual representation element has no name, the keyword *rep*can be omitted.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefC{ 
attributex:Real; 
assertx_constraint{ 
repinOCLlanguage"ocl"/* self.x > 0.0 */ // named textual representation 'inOCL' for language 'ocl' 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An annotating element is used to provide additional information about annotated elements. There are several kinds of annotating elements:</p><ul><li><strong>comment</strong> is used to provide textual descriptions about other elements. Comments may be about the namespace that owns them, or they may be about different elements, in which case they must be specified.</li><li><strong>documentation</strong> is a kind of comment used to document the annotated element. Documentation comments always annotate a single element, which is their owning element.</li><li><strong>textual representation </strong>is an annotating element whose textual body provides a representation of the annotated element in a specifically named language (such as SysML textual notation). As for documentation, a textual representation is always owned by its represented element.</li></ul><p>The annotated elements can own annotating elements, related via the owning membership relationship. The <strong>comment</strong> and <strong>textual representation </strong>elements can also be related to the annotated elements via the annotation relationship. In symbolic views, you can create or display the annotation and owning membership relationships using the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> command between the annotating and annotated elements, which can be represented either as element symbols or compartment elements. The <strong>documentation</strong> element is always related via the owning membership, however, if it is documenting an element other than its owner, the relationship is represented in a view as an annotation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50d59af8-4bc5-4c10-bb8b-e09aab5fc89a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a line note in the Textual Editor, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Commenting in the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating comments</h3><h4>Creating comments via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create comments via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and do one of the following: <ol><li>For an unnamed and/or unspecified comment, use symbols /* and */ with the comment text in between.</li><li>For a named and/or specified comment, declare the keyword <em>comment</em>, specify its name (optional), then the keyword <em>about </em>followed by the name of the element it annotates (optional), specify the keyword <em>locale</em> followed by the language of the body text and the region and/or encoding, then symbols /* and */ with the comment text in between.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9cde6d7-6322-4532-ac51-4daaedb71db2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */                                                                                     // unnamed comment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">comment </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">about</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle </span>/* May require revision */                                              // unnamed comment about the element 'vehicle' <br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">comment </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">revisions </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">locale</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;en_US&quot; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */     // named comment 'revisions' about the element 'vehicle' with specified locale (in English, region US)</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating comments via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create comments via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>dialog, click <strong>comment</strong>.</li><li>Specify the comment text.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying the commented element"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the commented element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating comments via the view palette</h4><p>To create comments via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Common group, click the <strong>comment </strong>button.</li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the comment symbol.</li><li>Specify the comment text.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying the commented element"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the commented element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3>Specifying the commented element</h3><h4>Specifying the commented element via the Textual Editor</h4><p>If the comment is an owned member of a namespace, then the explicit identification of annotated elements can be omitted, in which case the annotated element is implicitly the containing namespace. </p><p>To specify the commented element explicitly, use the <em>about</em> syntax. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating comments via the Textual Editor" />.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f6b8e6-8850-4aed-8c7b-671b4a343fa1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">comment </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">about</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle </span>/* May require revision */                    // unnamed comment about the element 'vehicle' <br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">comment </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">revisions </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">about</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */     // named comment 'revisions' about the element 'vehicle' </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying the commented element via the view palette</h4><p>To specify the commented element via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the <strong>annotation </strong>button.</li><li>In the view pane, click on the comment element, then the element you want to annotate with the comment. <br />The annotation relationship is created and the element is annotated with the comment.</li></ol><h4>Specifying the commented element via the Create Relation command</h4><p>To specify the commented element via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the comment element.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>annotation</strong>. The <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to annotate. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to annotate and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to annotate from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h3>Creating documentation</h3><h4>Creating documentation via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create documentation via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword <em>doc</em>, then specify the name (optional), followed by symbols /* and */ with the documentation text in between.   </li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="566f3f84-6f30-41b7-b67e-037eb961d0b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">doc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* tank is full */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating documentation via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create documentation via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element for which you want to create documentation, and in the shortcut menu, click<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>dialog, click <strong>documentation</strong>.</li><li>Specify the documentation text.</li></ol><h4>Creating documentation via the view palette</h4><p>To create documentation via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Common group, click the <strong>documentation </strong>button.</li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the documentation symbol.</li><li>Specify the documentation text.</li></ol><h4>Creating documentation via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create documentation via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li>See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the documentation of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying the documentation of the element via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><h3>Creating textual representation</h3><h4>Creating textual representation via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create textual representation via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword <em>rep</em>, then specify the name (optional), then the keyword language with the language specified as a literal string, followed by symbols /* and */ with the documentation text in between.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2985d8f2-a92b-458c-8dc7-5baacd46c271"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the textual representation element has no name, the keyword <em>rep </em>can be omitted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17abf92a-20b8-494d-8c07-ad9216e36b64"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">C</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">x</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">assert</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">x_constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rep</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> inOCL </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">language</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;ocl&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* self.x &gt; 0.0 */    // named textual representation 'inOCL' for language 'ocl' </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577869 space=SYSML2P version=13 -->
## PAGE 00487: Applying style sheets

- page_id: `249577869`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577869/Applying+style+sheets
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:36:49.047+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Whether you're using predefined style sheets provided by the modeling tool or custom ones you've created, you can manage which style sheets are applied to the entire project and which for specific views. This flexibility allows you to combine multiple style sheets based on your modeling needs, for example, applying a global style sheet to the entire project while applying distinct style sheets to individual views. Additionally, style sheets applied at both the project and view levels can be prioritized by setting their order of application. You can also disable project-level style sheets for a specific view.

Style sheets can be applied to:

- 
- 

##### Style sheets application priority order

The order of priority in which the style sheets are applied to views:

1. The configurations for applying style sheets to individual views are of the highest priority and thus higher than applying them to the entire project.
2. The ordering of style sheets for both individual views and the entire project is taken into account. If you specify multiple style sheets, order them based on your preferred priority, as the style sheets higher on the list take precedence over those lower on the list. Applying multiple style sheets is useful if the prioritized style sheet does not cover all available symbols. In that case, the secondary style sheet will cover those not covered by the first (if it contains the necessary rules).
3. The configuration for applying style sheets to the entire project is considered last.

##### Applying style sheets to the project

Applying style sheets to the project via the **Style Sheets** button****automatically creates a new package *ProjectStyleConfig* with the applied project style sheets configuration. The package is created in a root namespace dedicated to configurations that activate , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom creation dialogs' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling validation suites' space='']. If no such namespace exists in the model, it is automatically created with the *ProjectStyleConfig*package. The root namespace's name depends on the name of the package first created in the namespace.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

There must always be at least one style sheet applied to the project. If no style sheet is applied by the user, the default applied project style sheet is *DefaultStyleSheet*.

###### Applying style sheets to the project

To apply style sheets to the project

1. Click the Style Sheets button in the view toolbar. In the dropdown menu, click Project Style Sheets command. The Project Style Sheets dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Project Style Sheets command.png']
2. On the left side panel of the **Project Style Sheets**dialog, select the style sheet(s) you want to use and click the **Add [IMAGE alt='' src='']**button.
3. Select a style sheet on the right side of the panel and click the **Up**or **Down**buttons to change the selected style sheet's priority.
4. Click the OK button. The selected style sheets are applied and element symbols are updated on project view s.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Applying style sheets to a specific view

To apply style sheets to a specific view, the view must be defined by a symbolic view definition.Symbolic views containing *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram* or *Views::asTreeDiagram* renderers, but not defined by symbolic view definitions, are automatically defined by *DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv* once you .

###### Applying style sheets to a specific view

To apply style sheets to a specific view

1. Open the specific view whose style sheets you want to modify.
2. Click the Style Sheets button in the view toolbar . In the dropdown menu, click Apply Style Sheets command. The Apply Style Sheets dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Apply Style Sheets command.png']
3. On the left side panel of the **Apply****Style Sheets**dialog, select the style sheet(s) you want to use and click the **Add [IMAGE alt='' src='']**button.
4. Select a style sheet on the right side of the panel and click the **Up**or **Down**buttons to change the selected style sheet's priority.
5. (Optional) Select the check box for the Ignore Active Project Style Sheets option. Selecting the option ignores the specification of applied project style sheets specified in the **Project Style Sheets** dialog for the selected view, only taking into account the style sheets specified for this exact view in the **Apply** Style Sheets dialog .
6. Click the OK button. The selected style sheets are applied, and element symbols are updated in the view .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying style sheets applied to a specific view

To display style sheets applied to a specific view

1. Open the specific view.
2. Click the Style Sheets button in the view toolbar . In the dropdown menu, click Display Applied Style Sheets command. [ATTACHMENT filename='Display Applied Style Sheets command.png'] Part definition element symbols of the style sheets applied to the view are displayed in the view. If style sheets are applied to the entire project, but none for the specific view, the command will not display the style sheet symbols.
