<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576919 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00504: Command Palette

- page_id: `249576919`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576919/Command+Palette
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:14:40.629+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with Textual Editor > Managing Textual Editor > Textual Editor features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to using commands listed in the Textual Editor shortcut menu, you can use the Command Palette, which contains a multitude of additional commands.You can execute a command by clicking on it in the palette or by using shortcut keys if any are assigned.

##### Invoking the Command Palette

To invoke the Command Palette

Do one of the following:

- Right-click anywhere in the Textual Editor. In the shortcut menu, click the Command Palette command.
- Press F1 on the keyboard.

##### Quick-access symbols table

The usage of the Command Palette and the content displayed in it depends on the first symbol (the quick-access symbol) used in the Command Palette's search field.

| Symbol | Name | Description and Usage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| : | Go to Line/Column | This symbol is used to navigate the cursor to a specific line. Writing the colon in the search field displays the number of the line in which the cursor is positioned at the time. To access a specific line, type its number after the colon, e.g., ':22'.Shortcut keys: Ctrl+G |
| @ | Go to Symbol | This symbol is used to navigate to a navigate to a specific element by its name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation, e.g. '@requirement'. Shortcut keys: Ctrl+Shift+OUse up/down keyboard keys to navigate the displayed search results. Each is highlighted in the textual notation for easy navigation. |
| @: | Go to Symbol by Category | This symbol is used to sort elements by their assigned categories (properties, modules, methods, etc.) |
| > | Show and Run Commands | This symbol is used to display a large selection of various commands you can execute on the textual notation where the cursor is positioned. To search for a specific command type its name after the > symbol, e.g., '>fold'.This is the default quick-access symbol that is displayed in the search field once the Command Palette is displayed. Useful commands'Toggle High Contrast Theme' - switches the editor to dark mode. |
| (empty) | - | If the search field is empty, select the relevant symbol from the displayed menu containing all quick-access symbols. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In the shortcut menu, click the Command Palette command.</li><li>Press F1 on the keyboard.</li></ul><h3>Quick-access symbols table </h3><p>The usage of the Command Palette and the content displayed in it depends on the <u>first</u> symbol (the quick-access symbol) used in the Command Palette's search field.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Symbol</th><th>Name</th><th>Description and Usage</th></tr><tr><td>:</td><td>Go to Line/Column</td><td><p>This symbol is used to navigate the cursor to a specific line. Writing the colon in the search field displays the number of the line in which the cursor is positioned at the time. To access a specific line, type its number after the colon, e.g., ':22'.</p><p><strong>Shortcut keys:</strong> Ctrl+G</p></td></tr><tr><td>@</td><td>Go to Symbol</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>This symbol is used to navigate to a navigate to a specific element by its name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation, e.g. '@requirement'. </p><p><strong>Shortcut keys: </strong>Ctrl+Shift+O</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68742d22-1498-4fca-b08a-606f64a9f7a0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use up/down keyboard keys to navigate the displayed search results. Each is highlighted in the textual notation for easy navigation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>@:</td><td>Go to Symbol by Category</td><td><p>This symbol is used to sort elements by their assigned categories (properties, modules, methods, etc.)</p></td></tr><tr><td>&gt;</td><td>Show and Run Commands</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>This symbol is used to display a large selection of various commands you can execute on the textual notation where the cursor is positioned. To search for a specific command type its name after the &gt; symbol, e.g., '&gt;fold'.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d05c3477-1a8e-4279-a5de-035da62fffd8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This is the default quick-access symbol that is displayed in the search field once the Command Palette is displayed. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="635fa275-68e9-4fa1-b123-8cbbbe770137"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful commands</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>'Toggle High Contrast Theme' - switches the editor to dark mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>(empty)</td><td>-</td><td><p>If the search field is empty, select the relevant symbol from the displayed menu containing all quick-access symbols.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577067 space=SYSML2P version=17 -->
## PAGE 00506: Compartments

- page_id: `249577067`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577067/Compartments
- version_number: 17
- version_date: `2026-06-18T10:54:06.157+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with views > Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A compartment view usage is an area on the element symbol that displays specific element data. You can also use compartments to. Any usage or definition element may have compartments listing theirownedand inherited elements. The element symbol compartments are a graphical display of the element body in the textual notation.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

requirementdefMassLimitationRequirement{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be less than or equal to the required mass. */ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassReqd:MassValue; 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=massReqd 
} 
}

###### On the left: the requirement symbol with *documentation*, *attributes*, and *require constraints* compartments. 
On the right: the same requirement with its owned elements declared in the body.

##### Displaying and suppressing compartments

Many symbols have various compartments available to them which are hidden by default. Follow the the options listed below to check it if the needed compartment is not displayed.

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- In the view, click the symbol, then click the **Compartments**button. Select or deselect the particular compartment. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the view, right-click the symbol and in its shortcut menu, click Symbol Style . Then enable or disable the Show Compartment property for the needed compartment in the **Symbol Style**dialog .
- In the view, select a symbol and click the Suppress <compartment name> button. [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_compartment.png']

##### Displaying or hiding elements in compartments

Use the **Compartment Edit**dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in symbol compartments.

To open the **Compartment Edit** dialog

- Select a symbol and click the Compartments button. In the submenu, click the arrow to expand and then click Edit Compartments . [ATTACHMENT filename='compartment_edit_dialog.png']

##### Creating elements via compartments

You can use compartments to create new owned elements for the selected element.

To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_an_element_via_the_element_symbol_in_a_diagram.png']
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the compartment of the element kind you want to create, click the Create <element kind> button. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_an_element_via_compartment_in_a_diagram.png']

##### Compartments table

The table below lists the compartments available to specific element symbols based on their element metaclass.

| Compartment | Element metaclass |  | Compartment | Element metaclass |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| metadata | All namespaces |  | perfom actions | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionStateDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageStateUsage |
| doc | PackageDefinitionsUsages, excluding:IfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |  | subject | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| actors | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | stakeholders | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| objective | CaseUsageCaseDefinition |  | include use cases | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageExhibitStateUsageIfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |
| parameters | ConstraintUsageConstraintDefinitionActionUsageActionDefinition |  | frames | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| satisfy requirements | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |  | exhibit states | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| require constraints | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | attributes | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| assume constraints | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | items | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages |
| assert constraints | PartUsagePartDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | enums | EnumerationDefinition |
| constraints | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | actions | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| parts | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageIfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |  | ends | ConnectorAsUsageConnectionDefinition |
| ports | PartUsagePartDefinitionPortUsagePortDefinition |  | members | Package |
| while condition | WhileActionUsage |  | features (name not displayed in compartment) | MetadataUsage |
| until condition | WhileActionUsage |  | rules | Part usage or definition specializing Policies::StyleSheet |
| for iterator | ForLoopActionUsage |  | result | CalculationUsageCalculationDefinition |
| if condition | IfActionUsageElseAction |  | filters | PackageViewUsage ViewDefinition |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

have various compartments available to them which are hidden by default. Select or deselect the particular compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments_button.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the view, right-click the symbol and in its shortcut menu, click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Symbol Style</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Then enable or disable the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Show Compartment</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> property for the needed compartment </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">in the <strong>Symbol Style </strong>dialog</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>In the view, select a symbol and click the <strong>Suppress &lt;compartment name&gt;</strong> button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="suppress_compartment.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Displaying or hiding elements in compartments </h3><p>Use the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in symbol compartments.</p><p>To open the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a symbol and click the <strong>Compartments </strong>button. In the submenu, click the arrow to expand and then click <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartment_edit_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3><span>Creating elements via compartments </span></h3><p>You can use compartments to create new owned elements for the selected element.</p><p><span>To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select an element symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_an_element_via_the_element_symbol_in_a_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select an element symbol and on the right side of the compartment of the element kind you want to create, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>&lt;element kind&gt; </span></strong>button. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_an_element_via_compartment_in_a_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Compartments table</h3><p>The table below lists the compartments available to specific element symbols based on their element metaclass.</p><table class="wrapped <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577380 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00507: Concerns

- page_id: `249577380`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577380/Concerns
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-10-10T15:15:42.816+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A concern is used to specify the concerns of one or more stakeholders.One or more concerns can then be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Frame concern' space=''] in other requirement definitions and usages.

##### Creating concern usage and definition elements

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *concern def*for a concern definition.
  2. *concern* for a concern usage.
2. Name the element.
3. Specify the element in the element body. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] page.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

concerndefClientConcerns{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
stakeholderclient:Clients; 
doc/* Client concerns regarding the vehicle mass, functionality, and design. */ 
} 
concernmassConcern:ClientConcerns{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
stakeholderclient1:Clients; 
requireconstraint{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be equal to the required mass. */ 
} 
}

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. concern def to create a concern definition element.
  2. concern to create a concern usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the concern. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the procedures on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] page.

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create concern usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **concern** def button to create a concern definition element.
  2. The **concern** button to create a concern usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **concern**or the **concern****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the concern symbol.
3. Specify the concern. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577431 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00508: Connection

- page_id: `249577431`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577431/Connection
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-10-07T13:07:15.842+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A connection definition is a relationship that classifies a connection between related things, such as items and parts. A connection usage relates features to connector ends from the connection definition via a reference subsetting relationship, associating those ends with the specific usage elements that are to be connected. Connections can be either binary, with two connection ends, or n-ary, with multiple ends. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='N-ary connection' space=''] page.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross features' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *pressureSeatConn* connection connector where *bead* reference subsets *t.bead* connecting to *mountingRim*which reference subsets *w.rim*. 
Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating connection usage and definition elements

###### Creating connection usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create connection usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] (optional), then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button. connectiondefPressureSeat{ 
endpartbead:TireBead; 
endpartmountingRim:TireMountingRim; 
} 
partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partt:Tire[1] { 
partbead:TireBead[2]; 
} 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
}// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:connectt.beadtow.rim;// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list:connect(t.bead,w.rim); 
// unnamed unspecialized connection usage with named ends connecting featuresin a comma-separated list:connect(beadEnd ::>t.bead,rimEnd ::>w.rim);// unnamed specialized (feature typing) connection usage connecting featureswith explicit declaration: connection:PressureSeatconnectt.beadtow.rim;// named unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:connectionpressureSeatConnconnectt.beadtow.rim;// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting featureswith explicit declaration:connectionpressureSeatConn: PressureSeat connect bead ::> t.bead to mountingRim ::> w.rim; 
// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list:connectionpressureSeatConn:PressureSeatconnect(bead::>t.bead,mountingRim::>w.rim);} 
}

###### Creating connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the connection command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a connection. A connection path is created between the elements.

###### Creating connection usage elements via the Create Relation command

To create connection usage elements via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click connection . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Creating connection usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create connection usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create a connection definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **connection** def button to create a connection definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the connection definition symbol to be created.
- To create a connection usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **connection** button to create a connection usage.
  2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. A connection symbol is created between the elements. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection, n-ary connection**or the **connection****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating connection definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create connection definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword connection def .
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] of the needed element.
3. Click the Synchronize button. connectiondefPressureSeat{ 
endpartbead:TireBead; 
endpartmountingRim:TireMountingRim; 
}

###### Creating connection definition elements via the Create Element command

To create connection definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click connection def for connection definition.

###### Auto-creating connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create a connection definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A connection definition is a relationship that classifies a connection between related things, such as items and parts. A connection usage relates features to connector ends from the connection definition via a reference subsetting relationship, associating those ends with the specific usage elements that are to be connected. Connections can be either binary, with two connection ends, or n-ary, with multiple ends. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="N-ary connection" /></ac:link> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17973459-957d-4107-8aaa-02a3ac9cbe62"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The kind keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_connection.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The image displays a <em>pressureSeatConn</em> connection connector where <em>bead</em> reference subsets <em>t.bead</em> connecting to <em>mountingRim </em>which reference subsets <em>w.rim</em>.<br />Additionally, the connector is also <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></h6><h3>Creating connection usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating connection usage elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">connection</em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword.</span></li><li>Specify the element name (optional),<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>(optional), then state the features explicitly (using the <em>connect...to </em>syntax<span>) </span>or in a comma-separated list of related features.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0af6619-dda5-4ca5-8208-158cc9996ff5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>connection<span> </span></em>can be omitted.</li><li>You can also name connection ends, <span>associating them to features via </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PressureSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>connect<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">t</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead,</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;     </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>connection </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via a connection. A connection path is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage elements <span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>connection</strong>. The <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create connection usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create a connection definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>connection </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create a connection definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the connection definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create a connection usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>connection </strong></span><span>button to create a connection usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. A connection symbol is created between the elements.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>connection, n-ary connection </strong></span>or the <span><strong>connection </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4>Creating connection definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create connection definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword<span> </span><em>connection def</em><span>.</span></li><li>Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the needed element. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00fd817-e309-4d8e-839d-5b21596e9da9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PressureSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireBead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating connection definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create connection definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, click <strong>connection def</strong> for connection definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create a connection definition element via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>
````
